commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulcher, in which was never man yet laid. Cornelius a Lapide Now in the place where He was crucified there was a garden (for where could Jesus, the Author of all verdure and vigour, be buried, except in a garden. ) See notes on Matthew 27:60. And in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid; as S. Augustine says, "As no one before or after Him was conceived in a virgin"s womb, so no one either before or after Him was buried in that tomb.

commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: And if any one choose to give an additional meaning to the saying that the sepulchre was a new one, and that no man had been lain therein, be it so. He says, then, we may suppose, that the sepulchre was new, and that no one had been ever laid therein, that no one might be thought to have arisen from the sleep of death save Jesus only.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: This is added, lest it should be said, that it was not Christ, but some other, that rose from the dead; or at least, that he rose by the virtue of some other person reposing there. (Calmet)
